heroDesc: Additive manufacturing for rapid validation, complex geometry and low-volume parts
bodyTitle: 3D Printing Service from model review to post-processed delivery
bodyDesc: 3D printing service is suitable for rapid prototypes, complex geometry, low-volume parts and internal channels or lattice structures that are difficult to machine conventionally. Material and process choices differ in strength, accuracy, surface and cost, so review starts with intended use before selecting resin, nylon, metal or another route.

panels.title: Use Case
panels.subtitle: Validation / Complex Geometry
panels.body: Good for visual checks, assembly validation, complex channels and lightweight structures.

panels.title: Material Choice
panels.subtitle: Use-Case Driven
panels.body: Appearance, strength, heat resistance and cost affect process selection.

panels.title: Post-Processing
panels.subtitle: Cleaning / Blasting / Dyeing
panels.body: Surface texture and support marks differ by process and need specific treatment.

tableRows: Thin features and trapped powder need review
Material performance depends on process and grade
Reviewed by process, size and material
Support removal, cleaning, blasting, dyeing, heat treatment
Surface result should be confirmed early

steps.title: Model check
steps.body: Check file integrity, wall thickness, holes, supports and powder removal paths.

steps.title: Process and material selection
steps.body: Match route by use case, strength, appearance and budget.

steps.title: Printing and cleaning
steps.body: Print, remove supports, depowder, cure or heat treat as needed.

steps.title: Post-process delivery
steps.body: Complete blasting, dyeing, sanding or local post-machining as required.

asideCta.title: Submit Requirements
asideCta.body: Upload drawings and project goals so engineers can review the service route.

related.title: Related Service Combinations
related.desc: Complex projects often combine multiple services. Review by geometry, volume, material and delivery goals.

items.title: CNC Machining Service
items.body: Milling, turning, mill-turn and hole-pattern machining for metal and engineering plastic parts.

items.title: Molding Service
items.body: Injection molding, tool trials, low-volume molding and design optimization for plastic parts.

items.title: Custom Manufacturing Service
items.body: Coordinates CNC, sheet metal, finishing, purchased parts and assembly for complex projects.

faq.title: 3D Printing Service FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is 3D Printing Service suitable for?
items.a: 3D Printing Service is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a 3D Printing Service quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for 3D Printing Service?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
